It is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and held the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following two deities: * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
The term "Mikumari" means "water distribution," and the enshrined deities are considered guardians of irrigation and water flow from {{ill|Mount Kongō|en|Mount Kongō|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mount Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|WD=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}}. The shrine is one of the "Four Mikumari Shrines" in the Yamato Province, alongside {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|1=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}}, {{ill|Uda Mikumari Shrine|en|Uda Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3547711|lt=Uda Mikumari Shrine|1=Uda Mikumari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}. However, Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine holds a special status as the only one classified as a Myōjin-taisha in the Engishiki.
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. The first historical record of the shrine appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, which notes that in 840 CE (Shōwa 7), the "Mikumari deity" was elevated to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. Later, in 859 CE (Tian'an 3), the shrine's deity, named specifically as "Katsuragi Mikumari," was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. In the same year, offerings were made to the deity in response to prayers for rain.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Katsujō District, Yamato Province, as "Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine," designated as a Myōjin-taisha. It received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, as well as in rituals for rain and agricultural abundance.
=== Later History === Details of the shrine's medieval and early modern history are sparse. During the Edo period (1603–1868), disputes over water rights between Yamato Province and Kawachi Province were significant. A stone lantern dated to 1702 (Genroku 15) commemorates a victory in these disputes, and the shrine's annual festival on December 20 is thought to reflect the date of this resolution.
In the Meiji period (1868–1912), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Divine Ranks == * 840 CE (Shōwa 7), October 7: Promoted from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (Shoku Nihongi). * 859 CE (Tian'an 3), January 27: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku).

{{infobox shinto shrine |image = Katsuragi-mikumari-jinja honden.JPG |deity = * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity) |festival={{ill|December 20|en|December 20|WD=Q2455|lt=December 20|1=December 20}}}} '''Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine''' (葛木水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and held the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following two deities: * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
The term "Mikumari" means "water distribution," and the enshrined deities are considered guardians of irrigation and water flow from {{ill|Mount Kongō|en|Mount Kongō|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mount Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|WD=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}}. The shrine is one of the "Four Mikumari Shrines" in the Yamato Province, alongside {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|1=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}}, {{ill|Uda Mikumari Shrine|en|Uda Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3547711|lt=Uda Mikumari Shrine|1=Uda Mikumari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}. However, Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine holds a special status as the only one classified as a Myōjin-taisha in the Engishiki.
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. The first historical record of the shrine appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, which notes that in 840 CE (Shōwa 7), the "Mikumari deity" was elevated to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. Later, in 859 CE (Tian'an 3), the shrine's deity, named specifically as "Katsuragi Mikumari," was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. In the same year, offerings were made to the deity in response to prayers for rain.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Katsujō District, Yamato Province, as "Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine," designated as a Myōjin-taisha. It received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, as well as in rituals for rain and agricultural abundance.
=== Later History === Details of the shrine's medieval and early modern history are sparse. During the Edo period (1603–1868), disputes over water rights between Yamato Province and Kawachi Province were significant. A stone lantern dated to 1702 (Genroku 15) commemorates a victory in these disputes, and the shrine's annual festival on December 20 is thought to reflect the date of this resolution.
In the Meiji period (1868–1912), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Divine Ranks == * 840 CE (Shōwa 7), October 7: Promoted from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (Shoku Nihongi). * 859 CE (Tian'an 3), January 27: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku).

{{infobox shinto shrine |image = Katsuragi-mikumari-jinja honden.JPG |deity = * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity) |festival={{ill|December 20|en|December 20|WD=Q2455|lt=December 20|1=December 20}}}} '''Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine''' (葛木水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|WD=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|WD=Q9610964}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and held the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following two deities: * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
The term "Mikumari" means "water distribution," and the enshrined deities are considered guardians of irrigation and water flow from {{ill|Mount Kongō|en|Mount Kongō|WD=Q1348310|lt=Mount Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|WD=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}}. The shrine is one of the "Four Mikumari Shrines" in the Yamato Province, alongside {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|1=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}}, {{ill|Uda Mikumari Shrine|en|Uda Mikumari Shrine|WD=Q3547711|lt=Uda Mikumari Shrine|1=Uda Mikumari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}. However, Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine holds a special status as the only one classified as a Myōjin-taisha in the Engishiki.
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. The first historical record of the shrine appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|WD=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, which notes that in 840 CE (Shōwa 7), the "Mikumari deity" was elevated to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. Later, in 859 CE (Tian'an 3), the shrine's deity, named specifically as "Katsuragi Mikumari," was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. In the same year, offerings were made to the deity in response to prayers for rain.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Katsujō District, Yamato Province, as "Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine," designated as a Myōjin-taisha. It received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, as well as in rituals for rain and agricultural abundance.
=== Later History === Details of the shrine's medieval and early modern history are sparse. During the Edo period (1603–1868), disputes over water rights between Yamato Province and Kawachi Province were significant. A stone lantern dated to 1702 (Genroku 15) commemorates a victory in these disputes, and the shrine's annual festival on December 20 is thought to reflect the date of this resolution.
In the Meiji period (1868–1912), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Divine Ranks == * 840 CE (Shōwa 7), October 7: Promoted from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (Shoku Nihongi). * 859 CE (Tian'an 3), January 27: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku).

== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following two deities: * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
The term "Mikumari" means "water distribution," and the enshrined deities are considered guardians of irrigation and water flow from {{ill|Mount Kongō|en|Mount Kongō|qid=Q1348310|lt=Mount Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|qid=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}}. The shrine is one of the "Four Mikumari Shrines" in the Yamato Province, alongside {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|qid=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|1=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}}, {{ill|Uda Mikumari Shrine|en|Uda Mikumari Shrine|qid=Q3547711|lt=Uda Mikumari Shrine|1=Uda Mikumari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}. However, Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine holds a special status as the only one classified as a Myōjin-taisha in the Engishiki.
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. The first historical record of the shrine appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, which notes that in 840 CE (Shōwa 7), the "Mikumari deity" was elevated to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. Later, in 859 CE (Tian'an 3), the shrine's deity, named specifically as "Katsuragi Mikumari," was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. In the same year, offerings were made to the deity in response to prayers for rain.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Katsujō District, Yamato Province, as "Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine," designated as a Myōjin-taisha. It received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, as well as in rituals for rain and agricultural abundance.
=== Later History === Details of the shrine's medieval and early modern history are sparse. During the Edo period (1603–1868), disputes over water rights between Yamato Province and Kawachi Province were significant. A stone lantern dated to 1702 (Genroku 15) commemorates a victory in these disputes, and the shrine's annual festival on December 20 is thought to reflect the date of this resolution.
In the Meiji period (1868–1912), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Divine Ranks == * 840 CE (Shōwa 7), October 7: Promoted from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (Shoku Nihongi). * 859 CE (Tian'an 3), January 27: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku).
== Grounds == <gallery> File:Katsuragi-mikumari-jinja haiden.JPG|Worship Hall (Haiden) File:Katsuragi-mikumari-jinja torii.JPG|Torii Gate </gallery>

{{infobox shinto shrine |image = Katsuragi-mikumari-jinja honden.JPG |deity = * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|ja|水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|fr|Mikumari|sv|Mikumari|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|qq=}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity) |festival={{ill|December 20|en|December 20|qid=Q2455|lt=December 20|1=December 20}}}} '''Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine''' (葛木水分神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Gose, Nara|en|Gose, Nara|qid=Q847334|lt=Gose, Nara|1=Gose, Nara}}, Nara Prefecture, Japan. It is classified as a {{ill|Myōjin-taisha|ja|名神大社|fr|Myōjin Taisha|zh|名神大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōjin-taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Myōjin Taisha|qid=Q9610964}} under the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and held the status of a [[village shrine]] (村社) under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qid=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}.
== Enshrined Deities == The shrine venerates the following two deities: * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Ame-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|天水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Heavenly Water Distribution Deity) * {{ill|Mikumari|8=fr|lt=Kuni-no-Mikumari-no-Kami|ja|国水分神|1=Mikumari|2=ja|3=水分神|4=zh|5=水分神|6=sv|7=Mikumari-no-Kami|8=fr|9=Mikumari|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikumari|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 水分神}} (Earthly Water Distribution Deity)
The term "Mikumari" means "water distribution," and the enshrined deities are considered guardians of irrigation and water flow from {{ill|Mount Kongō|en|Mount Kongō|qid=Q1348310|lt=Mount Kongō|1=Mount Kongō}} and {{ill|Mount Katsuragi|en|Mount Katsuragi|qid=Q11620894|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Katsuragi}}. The shrine is one of the "Four Mikumari Shrines" in the Yamato Province, alongside {{ill|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|en|Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|qid=Q3529714|lt=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine|1=Yoshino Mikumari Shrine}}, {{ill|Uda Mikumari Shrine|en|Uda Mikumari Shrine|qid=Q3547711|lt=Uda Mikumari Shrine|1=Uda Mikumari Shrine}}, and {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|qq=}}. However, Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine holds a special status as the only one classified as a Myōjin-taisha in the Engishiki.
== History ==
=== Early History === The exact founding date of the shrine is unknown. The first historical record of the shrine appears in the {{ill|Shoku Nihon Kōki|en|Shoku Nihon Kōki|qid=Q1041301|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihon Kōki}}, which notes that in 840 CE (Shōwa 7), the "Mikumari deity" was elevated to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower. Later, in 859 CE (Tian'an 3), the shrine's deity, named specifically as "Katsuragi Mikumari," was promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower, according to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}. In the same year, offerings were made to the deity in response to prayers for rain.
The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} under Katsujō District, Yamato Province, as "Katsuragi Mikumari Shrine," designated as a Myōjin-taisha. It received offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, as well as in rituals for rain and agricultural abundance.
=== Later History === Details of the shrine's medieval and early modern history are sparse. During the Edo period (1603–1868), disputes over water rights between Yamato Province and Kawachi Province were significant. A stone lantern dated to 1702 (Genroku 15) commemorates a victory in these disputes, and the shrine's annual festival on December 20 is thought to reflect the date of this resolution.
In the Meiji period (1868–1912), the shrine was designated as a village shrine under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== Divine Ranks == * 840 CE (Shōwa 7), October 7: Promoted from unranked to Junior Fifth Rank, Lower (Shoku Nihongi). * 859 CE (Tian'an 3), January 27: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower (Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku).
